related_iss: ISS-1742 (closed 2026-04-30T05:23Z; this is a same-cycle precision tightening, not a re-open)
summary: Mode 2b verification of decider self-applied ISS-1742 closure (commit ac0e54f) reproduces the ~7-sec totality math at ω=0.25°/min × h_Moon=2,534 km — math is consistent with EXP-272 math_verification box and no cross-section refs need updating. However the new prose claim "still more than 20× short of the observed 138-second maximum" overshoots the actual ratio (19.3-19.6× depending on whether we use 1.30 or 1.32 km totality diameter). Curmudgeon recommendation in SEC-4.2.7-EXP272.c1.json explicitly said "~20× shortfall". One-substring fix restores the curmudgeon's phrasing: "more than 20×" → "close to 20×". See EXP-281.json for the patch spec, math, and four anticipated-objections analyses.
